nodes.title: ① Cinematic Prompt Expansion
widgets_values: A lone astronaut floating through a nebula

You are a cinematic video prompt engineer. Expand the user's brief idea into a detailed, vivid, production-ready video prompt. Use the structure: [Subject] + [Action] + [Scene] + [Camera Movement] + [Lighting] + [Style]. Output only the expanded prompt, no preamble, no explanation, no labels.

nodes.title: ② Refinement Pass
widgets_values: You are a senior director of photography. Take the given prompt and output ONLY the refined version — more specific about lighting ratios, camera lens type (e.g. '35mm anamorphic', '100mm macro'), film grain, color grading style (e.g. 'teal-and-orange', 'desaturated cold'), and depth of field. No preamble, no labels.

nodes.title:
③ Scene 1 — Nebula Entrance
(441 frames, cinematic slow pan)

④ Scene 2 — Black Hole Transition
(image-to-video, 241 frames)

⑤ Scene 2 Prompt Variant
(altered continuation)
widgets_values: You are a cinematic video prompt engineer. Take the given cinematic prompt and rewrite it for a DIFFERENT scene that CONTINUES the narrative. The new scene should transition from the original setting to a new one (e.g. nebula → black hole, city → space). Keep all the cinematic quality but change the subject, action, and environment. Use the structure: [Subject] + [Action] + [Scene] + [Camera Movement] + [Lighting] + [Style]. Output only the new prompt.

nodes.title: Note
values:
Multi-Scene Cinematic Pipeline

Flow:
  1. Agnes 2.0 Flash expands short prompt
  2. Refinement pass adds DOF, lens, grading
  3. Scene 1: Nebula entrance (441 frames, 18.4s)
  4. Scene 2: Black hole transition (241 frames, 10s)
  5. Scene 3: Cosmic climax (241 frames, 10s)

Total runtime: ~38 seconds

Each scene can use the previous scene's
last frame as image-to-video input.
Connect Scene 1 output → Scene 2 image,
Scene 2 output → Scene 3 image for seamless
transitions.

External concat (FFmpeg/QuickTime) → final film.

nodes.title: Note
values:
⚠️ Scenes are generated independently.
To chain them: extract last frame of
Scene N, paste as 'image' input of Scene N+1
for image-to-video continuity.

Or use Scene 1 (text-to-video) as source
for Scene 2 and 3 (image-to-video).

nodes.title: Pro Tips
values:
• Lower temperature (0.5) for refinement → more deterministic
• Higher temperature (0.8) for scene variants → creative diversity
• 441 frames = 18.4s, 241 frames = 10s, 121 frames = 5s
• num_frames must be 8n+1 and ≤ 441 (rule enforced client-side)

info.name: Multi-Scene Cinematic Pipeline (3 Scenes)
info.author: Agnes Workflow Generator
info.description: Advanced multi-scene video pipeline: expand → refine → generate Scene 1 (text-to-video, 441 frames) → generate Scene 2 (image-to-video, 241 frames) → generate Scene 3 (image-to-video, 241 frames). Total ~38 seconds. Scenes can be chained via last-frame extraction for seamless narrative transitions.
